eyebrow.zh: 数字公民破解 · 4 年级

chrome.sub: A student got added to a busy class group chat. Some messages were kind. Some were not. Crack four locks to learn how to be a good friend in a group chat.
chrome.briefP: You just joined a group chat with your whole class. People are spamming, forwarding private messages, and piling on one friend. Read the clues, then open all four locks. Each lock has a reason that shows why the answer is right.
chrome.footerText: Digital Citizenship Breakouts · Runs entirely in your browser — no logins, no data collected.
chrome.winP: You learned how to greet people when you join a chat, why forwarding a private message is wrong, how to stop a pile-on, how to disagree in a kind way, and the difference between muting and blocking. Great work, chat helper!

clues.title: Someone added you
clues.body: When you are added to a group chat, say a short, friendly hello. You do not need to send lots of messages at once. Sending message after message is called spam. Post once, then wait and read what others say.

clues.title: It was just for you
clues.body: A friend sent you a private message just for you. Do not forward it to the group chat. That message was not yours to share. Sharing private words breaks trust. Keep private messages private.

clues.title: Don't join the pile
clues.body: One person made a small mistake. Then lots of people started teasing them all at once. That is called a pile-on. Do not join in. Do not pile on. You can send a kind message or tell a trusted adult instead.

clues.title: Say it in a kind way
clues.body: You can disagree in a chat and still be kind. Use calm words, not mean names. Try 'I see it a different way' instead of 'You are wrong.' You can talk about the idea without hurting the person.

clues.title: Two quiet buttons
clues.body: When a chat gets too noisy, you can mute it. Mute hides the buzzes for a while, but you stay in the chat. Block is stronger. Block stops one person from messaging you at all. Mute for noise, block for someone unsafe.

clues.title: A fact from far away
clues.body: Fun fact: a day on the planet Venus is longer than its whole year. Cool, right? But this is just a space fact — it will not help you open any lock.

locks.title: You Got Added
locks.q: You were just added to a class group chat. What is the kind thing to do?
options: Send ten quick messages in a row so people notice you
Say one short, friendly hello, then read what others post
Forward a private message a friend sent you just for you
locks.reason: A short, friendly hello is polite. Sending message after message is spam, and forwarding a private message breaks a friend's trust.

locks.title: Be a Good Chat Friend
locks.q: Check EVERY choice that shows good manners in a group chat.

items.t: Sending a kind message to someone being teased
Piling on when one person makes a small mistake
Forwarding a private message to the whole group
Spamming the chat with message after message

locks.reason: Good chat friends disagree kindly and help someone being teased. Piling on, forwarding private words, and spamming all hurt the group.

locks.title: Too Much Noise
locks.q: The chat is too noisy, but no one is unsafe. To hide the buzzes and stay in the chat, you can ______ it.
locks.reason: Mute hides the noise while you stay in the chat. Blocking is stronger and is for stopping someone who is unsafe, not for noise.

locks.title: Stop the Pile-On
locks.q: One friend is getting piled on in the chat. Tap the steps in the RIGHT order.
locks.reason: First REFUSE to join the pile-on, then SUPPORT the friend with a kind message, then TELL a trusted adult. Refuse, Support, Tell.
